transcript: The TCP, IP, and OSI models are frameworks used for network standardization. The TCP, IP model consists of four layers, application, transport, internet, and network access. In contrast, the OSI model is a conceptual framework with seven layers, physical, data link, network, transport, session, presentation, and application. While the OSI model is used for theoretical abstraction and mapping, TCP, IP is the practical protocol suite used in the real world. For example, HTTP operates at the application layer, while TCP handles transport through encapsulation, ensuring data reaches the correct destination using IP at the internet layer.

Networking uses models like OSI and TCP IP so different computers can talk to each other. The OSI model is the long one with 7 layers like the physical and application layers. The TCP IP model is shorter and only has 4 layers. These models have with abstraction. You use protocols like HTTP for the web and IP for addresses. They are similar because they both have a transport layer but they map to each other differently depending on which one you are looking at.

The OSI model is how the internet works and it has about seven or eight layers I think. Then there is the TCP model which is basically the same thing but for actual hardware. These models are important for standardization so that cables work. An example of a protocol would be a website URL. The layers include things like the internet layer and the signal layer. It is a logical structure for data communication.
